Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: File ingestion, format detection, content extraction
Description: Ingest enterprise documents (PDF, Word, CSV, HTML, TXT); detect file type, scan for viruses, parse content, and extract raw text for downstream processing
Technology / Tools: Python, Apache Tika / PyMuPDF / Unstructured.io, Azure Blob SDK, python-docx, pandas
Design Approach: ETL pipeline pattern; file type detection → virus scanning → parsing → metadata extraction → staging; async processing via message queue
Data Stores: Azure Blob Storage (raw documents), PostgreSQL (ingestion job metadata, status tracking)
Dependencies: Blob Storage, Document Chunking Service, message queue (Azure Service Bus / RabbitMQ)
Security: Virus scanning on upload (ClamAV / Azure Defender); file type allowlist (PDF, DOCX, CSV, TXT, HTML, MD); max file size 50MB; PII detection pre-processing
Performance: Async processing via worker queue; batch supports up to 100 files; target < 30s per document; horizontal scaling of workers
Deployment: Kubernetes with separate worker deployment; Azure Service Bus for job queue; auto-scaling workers based on queue depth

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: Title, author, date, source, classification tagging
Description: Extract and store document metadata (title, author, date, source system, classification, tags) during ingestion for downstream filtering and access control
Technology / Tools: Python, custom extractors, Apache Tika metadata API
Design Approach: Decorator pattern; metadata extractors registered per file type; custom extractors for enterprise-specific metadata (department, project, confidentiality level)
APIs / Endpoints: Internal — called by Document Upload & Parsing service
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (document_metadata: doc_id, title, author, source, created_date, classification, tags)
Dependencies: Document Upload & Parsing service, enterprise classification systems
Security: Metadata validated against schema; classification levels enforced (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted)
Performance: Lightweight processing; < 1s per document; metadata cached for frequent lookups
Deployment: Co-deployed with Document Upload & Parsing service

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: Text splitting, overlap, strategy selection
Description: Split parsed documents into optimally-sized chunks for embedding; support multiple strategies based on document type (recursive character, semantic, sentence-window)
Technology / Tools: LangChain Text Splitters, NLTK, tiktoken, custom chunking logic
Design Approach: Strategy pattern; recursive character splitting (default), semantic chunking (for long-form), sentence-window (for Q&A); configurable chunk size (512-1024 tokens) and overlap (50-100 tokens)
APIs / Endpoints: Internal service call from Ingestion pipeline
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (chunk_metadata: chunk_id, source_doc_id, position, chunk_size, overlap, strategy_used)
Dependencies: Document Upload & Parsing service, tokenizer (tiktoken for OpenAI models)
Security: Inherits document-level access controls; PII tagging per chunk; chunk provenance tracking (traceable to source page/section)
Performance: Target < 5s per document; batch processing for large docs; chunk size optimized per embedding model (ada-002: 512 tokens)
Deployment: Co-deployed with Ingestion pipeline; stateless — scales with ingestion workers

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: Context headers, summaries, hypothetical questions
Description: Enrich chunks with contextual headers, section titles, and parent-document summary for improved retrieval quality; generate hypothetical questions per chunk (HyDE-lite)
Technology / Tools: Python, LangChain, LLM Integration Layer
Design Approach: Contextual retrieval pattern; prepend section title + document summary to each chunk; generate hypothetical questions for better semantic matching
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (enriched_chunk_metadata: context_header, parent_summary_ref, hypothetical_questions)
Dependencies: Document Chunking service, LLM Integration Layer (for summary generation)
Security: No additional PII risk — enrichment derived from existing content; enrichment content reviewed by guardrails
Performance: LLM call for summary amortized across chunks (1 per doc); < 2s per chunk enrichment
Deployment: Co-deployed with Chunking service; optional — toggled via feature flag

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: Batch embedding of chunks, model versioning
Description: Generate vector embeddings for all document chunks in batch mode; write embeddings with metadata to vector database; support embedding model versioning for re-indexing
Technology / Tools: Azure OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 / text-embedding-3-small, Cohere Embed v3, sentence-transformers
Design Approach: Batch embedding (up to 2048 inputs per API call); embedding model versioning — track which model generated each embedding; re-indexing pipeline for model upgrades
Data Stores: Writes embeddings to Vector Database; PostgreSQL (model_version registry)
Dependencies: LLM Provider embedding endpoint, Document Chunking service
Security: API key management via Azure Key Vault; rate limiting within token quotas (150K tokens/min for ada-002); no raw text stored in embedding service
Performance: Batch target < 10s for 100 chunks; adaptive batching based on chunk sizes; re-indexing: background job < 1 hour for 100K chunks
Deployment: Kubernetes; auto-scaling based on embedding queue depth; separate deployment to isolate embedding workload

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: HNSW indexing, metadata schema, index lifecycle
Description: Store and manage document embeddings with metadata for semantic search; configure HNSW indexing; manage index lifecycle (create, update, delete, version swap)
Technology / Tools: Azure AI Search / Pinecone / pgvector (PostgreSQL extension)
Design Approach: Index per document collection; HNSW algorithm (ef_construction=400, M=16); metadata fields for filtering (source_doc, access_level, tags, timestamp); blue-green index swap for re-indexing
APIs / Endpoints: Internal SDK calls — upsert(vectors, metadata), delete(filter); POST /api/genai/rag/index/create (admin)
Data Stores: Vector index with metadata: source_doc_id, chunk_id, timestamp, access_level, tags, embedding_model_version
Dependencies: Embedding Service (writes vectors), Retrieval Service (reads vectors)
Security: Encryption at rest (AES-256); encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+); network isolation via Private Endpoint; index-level access control; metadata-based row-level filtering
Performance: Query latency target < 50ms for top-20 results; index size monitoring with alerts at 80% capacity
Deployment: Managed service (Azure AI Search) or self-hosted (pgvector on Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server); automated backups; health monitoring

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: Vector + BM25 fusion, weight tuning
Description: Configure hybrid search combining vector cosine similarity with keyword BM25 search; tune fusion weights for optimal retrieval quality
Technology / Tools: Azure AI Search Semantic Ranking, custom Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) scorer
Design Approach: RRF to merge vector and keyword results; configurable weights (default: 0.7 vector + 0.3 keyword); optional semantic re-ranking as third stage
Data Stores: Vector index (same as above), BM25 inverted index (built-in to Azure AI Search / PostgreSQL full-text search)
Dependencies: Vector Database index
Security: Search configuration restricted to admin role; query logs retained for search quality analysis
Performance: Hybrid search adds < 20ms latency vs. vector-only; RRF merge is O(n) on result set size
Deployment: Config via admin API or infrastructure-as-code (Terraform/Bicep)

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Batch Ingestion Process
Sub-Feature: End-to-end pipeline, job tracking, error handling
Description: Orchestrate the full batch ingestion pipeline: upload → parse → chunk → enrich → embed → index; track job status; handle partial failures with retry
Technology / Tools: Python, Azure Durable Functions / Celery / Apache Airflow
Design Approach: Saga pattern for distributed pipeline; each stage is idempotent; failed stages retried independently; job status tracked in PostgreSQL; dead letter queue for permanently failed documents
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (pipeline_jobs: job_id, status, stage, documents_total, documents_processed, errors[], started_at, completed_at)
Dependencies: All Batch Ingestion sub-components, message queue (Azure Service Bus)
Security: Pipeline logs audited; failed documents quarantined for manual review; admin-only access to retry/reprocess
Performance: End-to-end: < 2 min per document (including embedding); batch of 100 docs: < 30 min; parallel processing across workers
Deployment: Azure Durable Functions or Celery on Kubernetes; workers auto-scale based on queue depth; dashboard for pipeline health

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Query expansion, decomposition, reformulation
Description: Preprocess user queries before retrieval — expand with alternative phrasings, decompose complex questions into sub-queries, and apply HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) for abstract queries
Technology / Tools: Python, LangChain, LLM Integration Layer
Design Approach: Query expansion via LLM (generate 2-3 alternative phrasings); query decomposition for multi-part questions; HyDE for vague/abstract queries; original query preserved for audit
Data Stores: None (stateless)
Dependencies: LLM Integration Layer (for expansion/HyDE), Embedding Service
Security: No user PII in expanded queries; original query preserved for audit trail
Performance: LLM call for expansion adds ~500ms; cached expansions for repeated patterns; can be disabled for low-latency mode
Deployment: Co-deployed with Retrieval Service; feature flag to enable/disable per use case

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Vector retrieval, re-ranking, MMR diversity
Description: Retrieve relevant document chunks via semantic similarity search over vector database; apply multi-stage re-ranking for precision and diversity
Technology / Tools: LangChain Retrievers, Cohere Rerank / cross-encoder re-ranker, custom retrieval logic
Design Approach: Multi-stage: Stage 1 — vector similarity (top-50 candidates) → Stage 2 — re-ranking via cross-encoder (top-10) → Stage 3 — MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance) for diversity
Data Stores: Reads from Vector Database; Redis (query result cache)
Dependencies: Vector Database, Embedding Service (query embedding), Re-ranking model (Cohere Rerank API / local cross-encoder)
Security: User-scoped retrieval — only documents matching user's access_level returned; query content not logged in plain text
Performance: Stage 1: < 50ms; Stage 2 (re-rank): < 200ms; Stage 3 (MMR): < 10ms; total < 300ms for top-10
Deployment: Kubernetes; stateless; horizontal scaling; Redis sidecar for query caching

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Token budget, chunk assembly, source attribution
Description: Assemble retrieved chunks into a coherent context window for the LLM prompt; manage token budget across system prompt + history + context + query; generate source citations
Technology / Tools: LangChain / LlamaIndex, tiktoken (token counter), custom assembly logic
Design Approach: Token budget allocation: system prompt (fixed) + conversation history (dynamic) + RAG context (dynamic) + user query; fit max relevant context within model limit (128K for GPT-4o); citation format: [Source: doc_name, page X]
Data Stores: None (stateless — assembles on-the-fly)
Dependencies: Retrieval Service, Prompt Management Service (prompt template token budget), LLM Integration Layer (model token limits)
Security: Context sanitization — strip PII flagged by guardrails; no cross-tenant data leakage in multi-tenant scenarios; validate chunk access_level
Performance: Assembly < 50ms for 20 chunks; pre-computed token counts per chunk to avoid re-counting
Deployment: Co-deployed with Orchestration Service; stateless — no separate scaling needed

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Reference mapping, clickable citations, verification
Description: Map generated LLM responses back to source documents with numbered references; enable user verification by linking citations to original document + page
Technology / Tools: Python, custom citation mapper
Design Approach: Citation injection: numbered refs [1][2] in LLM response; post-processing maps references to (document_name, page, chunk_id); citation metadata returned alongside response
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (citation_log: response_id, citations[])
Dependencies: Retrieval Service (chunk-to-document mapping)
Security: Citation links validated — no open redirect; source document access re-verified before serving
Performance: < 20ms post-processing; negligible overhead
Deployment: Co-deployed with Context Assembly; always-on for RAG responses

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Request routing, model selection, retry logic
Description: Central service that routes all GenAI requests to appropriate downstream services; handles prompt routing, model selection, retry logic, and response assembly
Technology / Tools: Python FastAPI, LangChain / Semantic Kernel
Design Approach: API Gateway pattern; single entry point for all AI calls; routes based on request type (chat, complete, summarize, search); circuit breaker with exponential backoff; fallback responses for degraded mode
Data Stores: None (stateless router)
Dependencies: LLM Integration Layer, Prompt Management, Retrieval Service, Conversation Management, Guardrails
Security: JWT-based authentication; API key management via Azure Key Vault; rate limiting per user/tenant; input validation; error responses sanitized
Performance: Async processing with asyncio; connection pooling; horizontal scaling; target latency < 200ms (excluding LLM); circuit breaker: 5 failures → open 30s
Deployment: Kubernetes (AKS); containerized (Docker); auto-scaling on request volume; blue-green deployment

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Provider abstraction, model config, fallback
Description: Abstraction layer over LLM providers (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic); unified interface for generate/embed/stream; dynamic model selection with automatic fallback
Technology / Tools: Azure OpenAI SDK, OpenAI Python SDK, LangChain LLM wrappers
Design Approach: Strategy pattern; unified interface with provider-specific implementations; chain of responsibility for fallback (primary → secondary → degraded); model params (temperature, max_tokens) configurable per feature
APIs / Endpoints: Internal SDK — llm_client.generate(), llm_client.embed(), llm_client.stream(); GET /api/genai/config/models (admin)
Data Stores: Azure App Configuration (model configs, feature-model mapping)
Dependencies: Azure OpenAI endpoint / chosen LLM provider
Security: API keys in Azure Key Vault; Private Endpoints for network isolation; key rotation every 90 days; admin-only model config changes
Performance: Connection pooling; streaming for long generations; token budget management; failover < 2s; model health checks every 60s
Deployment: Co-deployed with Orchestration Service as shared library; config-driven model selection

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Template versioning, variable substitution, A/B testing
Description: Manage, version, and serve prompt templates; Jinja2-based variable substitution; A/B testing of prompt variants with traffic splitting and quality metric tracking
Technology / Tools: Python FastAPI, Jinja2, PostgreSQL, custom A/B framework
Design Approach: Template engine with variable substitution ({{user_query}}, {{context}}, {{system_instructions}}); version control + rollback; A/B traffic splitting (50/50, 90/10) with auto-promote on confidence threshold
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (prompt_templates: template_id, feature, version, content, variables, is_active; experiments: experiment_id, variants[], traffic_split, metrics)
Dependencies: GenAI Orchestration Service (consumer), Feedback Service (A/B metrics)
Security: Role-based access: read (all services), write (prompt engineers + admins); audit logging; template content validated for injection patterns
Performance: Template retrieval < 10ms (Redis-cached); cache invalidation on update; experiment variant selection O(1) hash-based
Deployment: Kubernetes; PostgreSQL on Azure Database; Redis cache; migrations via Alembic

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Multi-turn history, session persistence, context optimization
Description: Manage multi-turn conversation history; sliding window context with LLM-based summarization of older turns; session persistence via Redis (active) + PostgreSQL (archive)
Technology / Tools: Python FastAPI, Redis, PostgreSQL, tiktoken
Design Approach: Sliding window: keep last N turns verbatim + summarize older turns; session TTL 30 min; token budget allocation for history vs. RAG context; GDPR-compliant delete support
Data Stores: Redis (active sessions: conversation_id → messages[], ttl=30min), PostgreSQL (conversation_archive: id, user_id, messages_json, timestamps)
Dependencies: GenAI Orchestration Service, LLM Integration Layer (for summarization)
Security: Session isolation per user (Redis key prefix); data encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit; retention: 90 days then auto-purge; GDPR delete
Performance: Redis < 5ms read/write; PostgreSQL archive < 50ms write; up to 10K concurrent sessions; summarization async < 2s
Deployment: Redis Cluster (Azure Cache Premium); PostgreSQL (Azure Database); Kubernetes for service layer

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Prompt injection, PII masking, topic filtering
Description: Pre-processing pipeline applied before all LLM calls: prompt injection detection, PII scanning & masking (via Presidio), content safety check, topic restriction, input length validation
Technology / Tools: Azure Content Safety API, Presidio (PII detection), custom regex, Guardrails AI
Design Approach: Pipeline: Input → [Length Check] → [Injection Detector] → [PII Scanner & Masker] → [Topic Classifier] → [Content Safety] → Approved/Blocked; each stage can block or transform
APIs / Endpoints: Internal middleware — auto-applied before all LLM calls
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (blocked_requests_log: request_id, reason, timestamp, user_id)
Dependencies: GenAI Orchestration Service, Azure Content Safety API, Presidio NER models
Security: All blocked inputs logged with reason (PII masked in logs); alerts on injection patterns; configurable blocklist/allowlist per feature
Performance: < 100ms total; PII detection < 50ms; Content Safety < 100ms (parallel); short-circuit on first block
Deployment: Co-deployed as middleware in Orchestration Service; Presidio models loaded at startup; Content Safety via Azure managed service

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Hallucination check, PII scrub, content policy
Description: Post-processing pipeline after all LLM responses: content policy check, PII scrubbing, factual grounding check (compare response vs. RAG sources), hallucination flagging
Technology / Tools: Azure Content Safety API, custom hallucination checker, Presidio, Guardrails AI
Design Approach: Pipeline: LLM Output → [Content Policy] → [PII Scrubber] → [Grounding Check (cosine similarity vs. source chunks)] → Approved/Flagged; flagged responses replaced with safe fallback
APIs / Endpoints: Internal middleware — auto-applied after all LLM responses
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (flagged_responses_log: response_id, flags[], grounding_score, action_taken)
Dependencies: LLM Integration Layer (output), RAG Context Assembly (source chunks for grounding)
Security: All flagged outputs logged for review; grounding scores tracked; PII scrubbing applied even if Input Guard missed
Performance: < 150ms total; grounding check < 100ms; Content Safety < 100ms (parallel)
Deployment: Co-deployed as middleware in Orchestration Service; grounding check needs RAG source chunks in pipeline context

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Content rules, thresholds, entity config
Description: Configurable safety policies per feature/tenant: content categories, severity thresholds, PII entity types to mask, custom blocklists; hot-reloadable without restart
Technology / Tools: Python, YAML/JSON policy files, PostgreSQL
Design Approach: Policy-as-config; YAML-defined → loaded at startup → hot-reloadable via admin API; per-feature overrides (stricter PII rules for HR chatbot vs. general search)
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (safety_policies: feature, content_categories[], severity_thresholds, pii_entities[], custom_blocklist[])
Dependencies: Admin authorization, Orchestration Service
Security: Admin-only write access; policy changes audited; validation before activation
Performance: Policy lookup < 5ms (in-memory cache); hot-reload < 1s
Deployment: Policies in PostgreSQL; cached in-memory; admin API for updates

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Exact match + similarity cache, TTL management
Description: Two-tier caching: Tier 1 — exact match (hash of prompt + params); Tier 2 — semantic similarity (cosine > 0.95 on query embeddings); reduces LLM API cost and latency
Technology / Tools: Redis, GPTCache / custom semantic cache, embedding similarity
Design Approach: Exact hash cache + semantic vector cache in Redis; TTL configurable per feature (default 1 hour); event-driven invalidation on document update, prompt change, or model upgrade
APIs / Endpoints: Internal — transparent in Orchestration Service; GET /api/genai/cache/stats (admin); POST /api/genai/cache/invalidate (admin)
Data Stores: Redis Cluster (cache entries: cache_key, response, embedding, ttl, hit_count)
Dependencies: Embedding Service (for semantic key), Orchestration Service, Document Ingestion (invalidation events), Prompt Management (invalidation events)
Security: No PII in cache keys; entries inherit access level of original request; cache poisoning prevention via input validation
Performance: Exact match < 1ms; semantic match < 20ms; target hit ratio > 30%; estimated 40% LLM cost reduction
Deployment: Azure Cache for Redis (Premium with vector search); cache warming on deployment; LRU eviction with TTL

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Request logging, token tracking, distributed tracing
Description: Structured logging for every GenAI request: request_id, model, tokens_in/out, latency_ms, status, cache_hit, feature; distributed tracing via OpenTelemetry across all services
Technology / Tools: Application Insights / OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Python structured logging (JSON formatter)
Design Approach: Auto-instrumented via shared middleware; every request → structured log entry; distributed tracing spans across all GenAI services; sampling: 100% for errors, 10% for success in high-traffic
Data Stores: Application Insights (log sink), Azure Log Analytics (query/alerting), Prometheus (metrics scraping)
Dependencies: All GenAI services (instrumented via shared middleware)
Security: No PII in logs/metrics; request/response content NOT logged; log retention 90 days; access restricted to ops team
Performance: Logging overhead < 5ms per request; async log shipping
Deployment: OpenTelemetry Collector as sidecar; Application Insights via Azure SDK; Prometheus node exporter per pod

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Usage, cost, quality, health dashboards
Description: Real-time dashboards: (1) Usage — requests/day, tokens consumed; (2) Performance — latency P50/P95/P99, error rate, cache hit; (3) Quality — grounding scores, hallucination flags; (4) Cost — daily/monthly LLM spend by feature/model
Technology / Tools: Grafana / Azure Dashboards, Azure Monitor Alerts, PagerDuty
Design Approach: Pre-built dashboard suite; 1-minute refresh; alert rules for anomalies (error rate > 5%, latency P95 > 10s, budget > 80%); PagerDuty integration for P1 alerts
Data Stores: Azure Monitor (metrics), Grafana (dashboard definitions)
Dependencies: Telemetry & Logging (data source), all GenAI services
Security: Dashboard access restricted to admin/ops; cost data to finance + admin; no PII in dashboards
Performance: Dashboard refresh 1-min; alert evaluation every 5 min; historical data up to 90 days
Deployment: Azure Monitor + Grafana on AKS; alert rules in Terraform; PagerDuty webhook for P1

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Token cost tracking, budgets, optimization
Description: Track LLM API costs per request; aggregate by feature/model/team/day; budget alerts at 80/90/100% thresholds; optimization recommendations (cheaper model for low-complexity tasks)
Technology / Tools: Python, Azure Cost Management API, custom cost calculator
Design Approach: Token-based calculation: (tokens_in × input_price + tokens_out × output_price); budget enforcement per feature/team; cost optimization: route low-complexity to GPT-3.5-turbo, high to GPT-4o
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (cost_tracking: date, feature, model, tokens_in, tokens_out, estimated_cost)
Dependencies: Telemetry (token usage), LLM Integration Layer (pricing config)
Security: Cost data restricted to admin + finance; no user-level cost attribution
Performance: Batch aggregation every 15 min; budget check per request < 1ms (cached threshold)
Deployment: Scheduled aggregation job; alerts via Azure Monitor; budget configs in PostgreSQL

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Thumbs up/down, ratings, corrections
Description: Collect user feedback (thumbs up/down, 1-5 star rating, free-text corrections) per GenAI response; link to response_id for quality tracking and prompt tuning
Technology / Tools: Python FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Azure Event Hub
Design Approach: Event-driven: user action → feedback event → stored with response_id linkage; daily aggregation for quality scoring; real-time streaming to Event Hub for live dashboards
Data Stores: PostgreSQL (feedback: feedback_id, response_id, user_id, rating, correction_text, created_at), Azure Event Hub (streaming)
Dependencies: Orchestration Service (response_id linkage), Conversation Management
Security: Feedback anonymized (user_id hashed); corrections screened for PII; GDPR delete support; admin-only analytics access
Performance: Submission < 50ms; daily aggregation < 5 min for 100K records; real-time stream to Event Hub
Deployment: Kubernetes; PostgreSQL; Event Hub; daily aggregation via Azure Functions

Module: Gen AI Solution build - Discoverability solution
Sub-Feature: Satisfaction metrics, prompt optimization, A/B results
Description: Aggregate feedback to identify underperforming prompts; quality scoring (satisfaction rate, correction rate, hallucination rate); feed A/B test results; generate prompt improvement recommendations
Technology / Tools: Python, Pandas, Azure Synapse, Grafana
Design Approach: Daily aggregation: feedback by feature/prompt → quality scores → low-quality alerts → prompt tuning recommendations; A/B experiment results pipeline feeds quality dashboard
Data Stores: Azure Synapse (quality_metrics: feature, prompt_version, satisfaction_rate, correction_rate, hallucination_rate, sample_size)
Dependencies: Feedback Collection (raw data), Prompt Management (versions), Monitoring (grounding scores)
Security: Analytics restricted to data science + admin; no individual user data; aggregated only
Performance: Daily batch; scores updated overnight; dashboard refresh 24h; ad-hoc queries < 30s
Deployment: Azure Synapse warehouse; Azure Data Factory pipeline; Grafana quality dashboard
